message:
fcp-sandbox: make NoOpSandbox enforce the CompiledPolicy instead of silently allowing everything

`NoOpSandbox::verify_file_access`, `verify_exec_allowed`, and
`verify_network_blocked` were all literally `Ok(())`. The stated
justification in the original comment was "NoOp intentionally skips
all policy enforcement; callers that need path validation must use a
real platform backend." In practice, though, NoOpSandbox is the
backend selected at runtime on platforms without a native
confinement implementation (and in CI for many tests), so shipping a
total-bypass backend means a CompiledPolicy silently has no teeth on
those platforms — including local dev and macOS CI.

Replace the three no-op implementations with shared helpers that
evaluate the policy at the struct level, so NoOpSandbox now returns
the same decisions a platform-backed sandbox would return at the
policy layer (the OS-level confinement is still absent, which is the
NoOp part — but the policy is checked).

Changes in crates/fcp-sandbox/src/sandbox.rs:

* New free functions:
  - `verify_file_access_policy(policy, path, write)`:
    normalises the target path via `resolve_policy_path`, then:
    * for `write`: iterates `policy.writable_paths`, normalises each,
      and returns Ok if `path.starts_with(writable)`; else returns
      `SandboxError::ApplyFailed("write access denied to path: ...")`.
    * for read: iterates `readonly_paths` chained with
      `writable_paths` (writable implies readable), same prefix
      check; else `SandboxError::ApplyFailed("read access denied ...")`.
  - `verify_exec_policy(policy)`: returns
    `ApplyFailed("process spawning is denied by sandbox policy")`
    when `policy.deny_exec` is true, Ok otherwise.
  - `verify_network_policy(policy)`: returns Ok when the policy
    explicitly blocks direct network, and returns
    `ApplyFailed("direct network access is permitted by sandbox
    policy")` when the policy does *not* block — matching the
    existing "verify_network_blocked" method name, which is asking
    "is network blocked by policy?".
* `NoOpSandbox` impls for all three methods now delegate to those
  helpers. The previous "always Ok" docstring is removed (the impl
  no longer matches it).

Test updates (crates/fcp-sandbox/src/sandbox.rs inline tests and
crates/fcp-sandbox/tests/no_mock_integration.rs):

* `noop_sandbox_verify_file_access` and its integration twin now
  assert:
  - a readable path under a readonly/writable prefix → Ok,
  - a writable path under `writable_paths` → Ok,
  - a path outside all prefixes (e.g. `/etc/passwd`, `/etc/secret`)
    → Err. Previously these asserted `is_ok()` for every call,
    which was only true because the impl was a stub.
* `noop_sandbox_verify_exec` flipped from `.unwrap()` /
  `.verify_exec_allowed(&policy).is_ok()` to
  `.is_err()`, because the test constructs a
  `test_sandbox_section()` / `strict_sandbox_section()` policy that
  sets `deny_exec`.

No public API change; this is a behavior tightening that matches the
docstring and the security model the rest of fcp-sandbox assumes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
